Opportunity Finder
AI-Driven Cybersecurity Spending Surge
Secular Trend High Conviction
AI is simultaneously creating more sophisticated cyber attacks (AI-generated phishing, deepfakes, autonomous malware) and enabling better defenses. Enterprise cybersecurity budgets are doubling as boards mandate AI-native security postures. The emergence of quantum computing as a near-term threat to encryption adds another layer of urgency.
FTNT
FTNT: Fortinet's custom ASIC-based firewalls (FortiGate) deliver 10-20x better price/performance than competitors relying on commodity hardware. Their FortiAI platform with 500+ AI patents provides AI-native threat detection. 55% global firewall market share and 70% Fortune 100 penetration create a massive recurring revenue base that grows as customers expand their security perimeters.
Identified: 2026-02-01 Timeframe: 12-36 months
AI Data Center Optical Interconnect Buildout
Supply Chain High Conviction
NVIDIA's next-generation GPU platforms (Blackwell Ultra, Rubin) require dramatically more bandwidth between chips, driving massive investment into optical interconnect technology. NVIDIA has publicly committed to co-packaged optics and active electrical cables, creating a multi-billion dollar TAM for suppliers of high-speed connectivity components.
CRDO MRVL
CRDO: Credo Technology is the leading supplier of Active Electrical Cables (AECs) for AI data centers, with 201% YoY revenue growth driven by hyperscaler demand. Their SerDes IP and AEC technology sit directly in NVIDIA's supply chain for rack-scale AI interconnects.
MRVL: Marvell Technology's custom silicon business (doubling to $1.5B in FY2026) designs the connectivity chips that go inside AI networking equipment. Their PAM4 DSPs and custom ASIC platform serve every major hyperscaler building AI infrastructure.
Identified: 2026-03-15 Timeframe: 12-24 months
Iran Ammonia Supply Shock
Supply Shock High Conviction
The Iran conflict has wiped out approximately 30% of global ammonia production capacity. With ammonia being a critical feedstock for nitrogen fertilizers, this supply shock creates an immediate pricing tailwind for surviving producers with existing capacity and distribution networks.
NTR
NTR: Nutrien is the world's largest crop inputs company with significant ammonia and nitrogen production capacity in Canada, far from the conflict zone. With existing infrastructure and distribution networks across North America, Nutrien is positioned to capture displaced demand and benefit from elevated fertilizer pricing.
Identified: 2026-04-01 Timeframe: 3-6 months
AI Power Bottleneck — Gas Turbines and Nuclear Booked Solid
Supply Chain High Conviction
Hyperscalers committed $630–700B in AI infrastructure capex for 2026, triggering an electricity demand surge that has sold out GE Vernova's gas turbine production slots through 2030. In Q1 2026, data center customers ordered more GE Vernova electrification equipment than in all of 2025 combined ($2.4B in a single quarter). The market is focused on chips and networking; the power infrastructure enabling them is less appreciated and increasingly scarce.
GEV CEG
GEV: GE Vernova is the dominant global gas turbine manufacturer with a backlog that expanded 20 GW in a single quarter to 100 GW — production is essentially sold out through 2030. New orders are being written at 10–20% higher prices per kW than Q4 2025. EBITDA margins are expanding from 8.4% (2025) toward 20% (2028) as higher-priced backlog flows through. At 43x forward P/E the stock is not cheap, but the visibility of a years-long sold-out order book makes it a rare durable earnings compounder in the industrial space.
CEG: Constellation Energy completed its $26.6B Calpine acquisition in January 2026, creating the nation's largest mixed nuclear-and-gas clean power portfolio at 60 GW. The 20-year Microsoft PPA for Crane Clean Energy Center (Three Mile Island restart) sets the template for hyperscaler clean-power contracts. At 19.8x forward P/E against 49–63% guided EPS growth in 2026 and a 20%+ base earnings growth trajectory through 2030, CEG offers the most attractive risk/reward in the AI power theme.
Identified: 2026-05-12 Timeframe: 12-24 months
Data Center Copper Demand Supercycle
Secular Trend On Our Radar
AI data centers consume 5-10x more copper per megawatt than traditional facilities due to high-density power distribution, cooling systems, and electrical infrastructure. With $500B+ in planned data center capex globally through 2028, copper demand from this sector alone could absorb a significant portion of global mine supply growth.
FCX
FCX: Freeport-McMoRan operates the Grasberg mine (world's largest copper/gold deposit) and has significant Americas-based production. With copper trading near all-time highs and Grasberg's underground ramp delivering higher grades, FCX offers direct leverage to the copper supercycle thesis. The company's U.S.-based smelter expansion also positions it for reshoring tailwinds.
Identified: 2026-03-01 Timeframe: 12-36 months
Aluminum Supply Shock — Gulf Smelters Stranded by Hormuz Closure
Supply Shock On Our Radar
The Strait of Hormuz closure (in effect since February 28, 2026) has stranded Gulf aluminum shipments from a region that produces ~10% of global aluminum output and exports ~20% of global raw aluminum supply. Analysts at Wood Mackenzie and Benzinga describe the disruption as 'the largest single supply shock a base metals market has suffered in the post-2000 era,' with approximately 3–3.5 million tonnes of annual supply effectively offline. Aluminum prices reached a four-year high of $3,571/MT in April 2026 with no near-term resolution in sight.
AA
AA: Alcoa is the largest North American aluminum producer, with operations in Canada and the US entirely outside the conflict zone. Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA rose $68M sequentially on higher aluminum prices alone, and net income more than doubled to $425M ($1.60/share). Trading at just 10.24x forward P/E in a price-elevated environment, the stock remains significantly discounted relative to its earnings power at current aluminum prices. The brownfield data center site monetization ($500M–$1B) provides a non-commodity catalyst that gives this beyond a pure cycle call.
Identified: 2026-05-12 Timeframe: 3-6 months
